label: Measurement time point of survey administration.
notes: Hidden control variable used to identify the measurement wave for repeated survey administrations.

label: Informed consent to participate in the research project.
values_coding: Y = consent provided; N = consent not provided
notes: Required consent item for study participation.

label: Randomization variable generated at baseline to assign study group.
values_coding: 1 = start with Phase A (AI coaching first); 2 = start with Phase B (baseline/transfer first)
notes: Hidden variable generated only at baseline (timePoint = 1); used to derive studyGroup.

label: Study group allocation indicating the order of study phases.
values_coding: 1 = Phase A first (AI coaching first); 2 = Phase B first (baseline/transfer first)
notes: Hidden control variable derived from randomGroup and used for wave-specific branching.

label: Hidden app label displayed in wave-specific app questions.
values_coding: "Gesundheits-App" = preinstalled health app; "Studien KI-App" = study AI app
notes: Hidden text variable that changes item wording depending on studyGroup and timePoint.

label: Self-reported number of days per week with vigorous physical activity.
values_coding: Integer number of days per week
notes: Vigorous activity is defined in the survey as activity that noticeably increases heart rate and induces sweating.

label: Self-reported minutes per day spent in vigorous physical activity.
values_coding: Integer number of minutes per day
notes: Displayed only if Bew1 is at least 1 day per week.

label: Self-reported number of days per week with moderate physical activity.
values_coding: Integer number of days per week
notes: Moderate activity refers to activities of moderate intensity as described in the survey.

label: Self-reported minutes per day spent in moderate physical activity.
values_coding: Integer number of minutes per day
notes: Displayed only if Bew3 is at least 1 day per week.

label: Self-reported number of days per week with light physical activity.
values_coding: Integer number of days per week
notes: Light activity refers to light-intensity activity as described in the survey.

label: Self-reported minutes per day spent in light physical activity.
values_coding: Integer number of minutes per day
notes: Displayed only if Bew5 is at least 1 day per week.

label: Stage of change regarding regular physical activity.
values_coding: PC = no regular physical activity and no intention to start within 6 months; CO = no regular physical activity but intention to start within 6 months; PR = no regular physical activity but intention to start within 30 days; AC = regular physical activity for less than 6 months; MA = regular physical activity for at least 6 months
notes: Single-item stage classification based on regular physical activity.

label: Hidden calculated total MET-minutes per week based on self-reported physical activity.
notes: LimeSurvey equation: METtotal = {((Bew1 * Bew2) * 9) + ((Bew3 * Bew4) * 5) + ((Bew5 * Bew6) * 3)} based on Nigg et al., (2024)

label: Hidden physical activity classification flag based on METtotal and stage of change.
notes: LimeSurvey equation: if((METtotal > 1500) and (STG == 'MA'), 1, 0). If a participant receives the PAFlag he is automaticaly excluded from the study.

label: Hidden subgroup classification for baseline physical activity level.
notes: LimeSurvey equation: if(METtotal < 650, 1, 2). Required for automatic T1 subgrouping. Subgroup 1 = inactive/lower METtotal than minimal WHO Guidelines, Subgroup 2 = METtotal between minimal and maximal WHO Guidelines.

label: My decision to be physically active is something I do automatically.
notes: Automaticity subscale item; scale score = mean of Habit1–Habit4; range 1–7.

label: My decision to be physically active is something I do without consciously remembering it.
notes: Automaticity subscale item; scale score = mean of Habit1–Habit4; range 1–7.

label: My decision to be physically active is something I do without thinking about it.
notes: Automaticity subscale item; scale score = mean of Habit1–Habit4; range 1–7.

label: My decision to be physically active is something I start doing before I realize that I am doing it.
notes: Automaticity subscale item; scale score = mean of Habit1–Habit4; range 1–7.

label: Intention to be physically active on most days during the coming week.
notes: TPB intention item; scale score = mean of Intention1–Intention3; range 1–7.

label: Intended effort to be physically active on most days during the coming week.
notes: TPB intention item; scale score = mean of Intention1–Intention3; range 1–7.

label: Plan to be physically active on most days during the coming week.
notes: TPB intention item; scale score = mean of Intention1–Intention3; range 1–7.

label: Evaluation of being physically active during the coming week as harmful versus beneficial.
notes: TPB attitude item; scale score = mean of Att1–Att5; range 1–7.

label: Evaluation of being physically active during the coming week as unpleasant versus pleasant.
notes: TPB attitude item; scale score = mean of Att1–Att5; range 1–7.

label: Evaluation of being physically active during the coming week as bad versus good.
notes: TPB attitude item; scale score = mean of Att1–Att5; range 1–7.

label: Evaluation of being physically active during the coming week as useless versus valuable.
notes: TPB attitude item; scale score = mean of Att1–Att5; range 1–7.

label: Evaluation of being physically active during the coming week as unenjoyable versus enjoyable.
notes: TPB attitude item; scale score = mean of Att1–Att5; range 1–7.

label: Perception that important others think the participant should be physically active on most days during the coming week.
values_coding: 1 = important others think I should not be physically active; 7 = important others think I should be physically active
notes: TPB injunctive norm item; scale score = mean of Norm1–Norm3; range 1–7.

label: Perception that regular physical activity on most days during the coming week is expected from the participant.
notes: TPB injunctive norm item; scale score = mean of Norm1–Norm3; range 1–7.

label: Perception that valued others would support the participant in being physically active on most days during the coming week.
notes: TPB injunctive norm item; scale score = mean of Norm1–Norm3; range 1–7.

label: Perception that most important others are physically active on many days.
values_coding: 1 = does not apply at all; 7 = fully applies
notes: TPB descriptive norm item; scale score = mean of Norm4–Norm6; range 1–7.

label: Perception that valued others are physically active on many days.
notes: TPB descriptive norm item; scale score = mean of Norm4–Norm6; range 1–7.

label: Perception that many similar others are physically active on many days.
notes: TPB descriptive norm item; scale score = mean of Norm4–Norm6; range 1–7.

label: Perceived possibility of being physically active on many days during the coming week.
notes: TPB perceived capability item; scale score = mean of Control1–Control2; range 1–7.

label: Perceived feasibility of being physically active on many days during the coming week if desired.
notes: TPB perceived capability item; scale score = mean of Control1–Control2; range 1–7.

label: Perceived amount of control over being physically active on many days during the coming week.
notes: TPB controllability item; scale score = mean of Control3–Control4; range 1–7. Overall perceived behavioral control can be averaged across capability and controllability.

label: Perception that being physically active on many days during the coming week mainly depends on oneself.
notes: TPB controllability item; scale score = mean of Control3–Control4; range 1–7. Overall perceived behavioral control can be averaged across capability and controllability.

label: Physical activity is enjoyable to the participant.
values_coding: 0 = does not apply at all; 4 = fully applies
notes: Motivation item (interest/enjoyment); subscale score = mean of IntVer1–IntVer3; range 0–4.

label: Physical activity is very interesting to the participant.
values_coding: 0 = does not apply at all; 4 = fully applies
notes: Motivation item (interest/enjoyment); subscale score = mean of IntVer1–IntVer3; range 0–4.

label: Physical activity is entertaining to the participant.
values_coding: 0 = does not apply at all; 4 = fully applies
notes: Motivation item (interest/enjoyment); subscale score = mean of IntVer1–IntVer3; range 0–4.

notes: Motivation item (perceived choice); subscale score = mean of wWahl1–wWahl3; range 0–4.

values_coding: 0 = does not apply at all; 4 = fully applies
notes: Motivation item (pressure/tension); subscale score = mean of DrAn1–DrAn3; range 0–4.

notes: Motivation item (pressure/tension); subscale score = mean of DrAn1–DrAn3; range 0–4.

values_coding: 0 = does not apply at all; 4 = fully applies
notes: Attention check item; exclude from subscale scoring.

notes: LimeSurvey equation: if((ACheck1 != 3) + (ACheck2 != 3) + (ACheck3 != 3) >= 2, 1, 0). Only relevant for T1.

label: Evaluation of whether the direct residential environment is densely built and highly sealed.
values_coding: 1 = fully applies; 2 = rather applies; 3 = partly/partly; 4 = rather does not apply; 5 = does not apply at all
notes: Subquestion of UmgebTyp; direct environment refers to approx. 800 m / 10 min walking distance.

label: Evaluation of whether the participant has quick access from home to nature such as forest, parks, or water.
values_coding: 1 = fully applies; 2 = rather applies; 3 = partly/partly; 4 = rather does not apply; 5 = does not apply at all
notes: Subquestion of UmgebTyp; quick access defined as less than 300 m / 5 min walking distance.

values_coding: 1 = fully applies; 2 = rather applies; 3 = partly/partly; 4 = rather does not apply; 5 = does not apply at all
notes: Subquestion of UmgebTyp.

label: Approximate distance from home to place of occupation.

label: Approximate distance from home to indoor physical activity opportunities.

label: Approximate distance from home to outdoor physical activity opportunities.
notes: Subquestion of EntfernPOI. Examples: park/forest, training field. If multiple places apply, participant should enter an average distance.
